response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack and systems software developer with a strong web, testing, and craftsmanship background, but the role demands principal-level hardware performance architecture expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in his history. His closest relevant work (low-latency DLang services, brief C/C++ work at National Instruments) is software performance engineering, not silicon or accelerator architecture, and there is no deep-learning workload experience at all. The years-of-experience bar is met, but the domain mismatch is fundamental.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Broad, long-tenured engineering background across web, embedded, and desktop with solid testing culture
Meets the raw experience requirement (BS + 8+ years) with 15+ years in software
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, training, or inference optimization background
No GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or accelerator performance analysis exposure
